skill.name: edge-computing-patterns
skill.description: Design and review edge runtime applications for Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge Functions, and Deno Deploy.
skill.summary: Plan low-latency edge applications with runtime constraints, caching patterns, middleware, authentication, and rate limiting.
skill.author: AI Agent Hub

security_audit.summary: Static external command and critical combination alerts are false positives from Markdown code fences, template literals, and a sentence that lists child_process as unavailable. The skill is documentation-only, with legitimate edge runtime examples that use fetch and environment bindings. No prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration, executable installer, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported command execution matches are Markdown code blocks, template literals, and a note that Node.js child_process is unavailable. No executable command invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are code fence delimiters, TypeScript examples, or text describing unavailable APIs. There is no script, shell command, or runtime hook in the skill.

low_findings.title: Network Calls Are Expected Edge Runtime Examples
low_findings.description: The fetch calls demonstrate proxying, authentication forwarding, rate limiting, and cache fill behavior. They do not target suspicious endpoints or send credentials to unknown domains.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network API usage is real, but it appears in instructional edge application examples. No evidence shows exfiltration, hidden endpoints, or malicious network behavior.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Usage Is Not Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The examples read VERCEL_REGION for metadata and JWT_SECRET for token verification. The values are not logged, transmitted to a third party, or written to storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment access is visible and limited to normal edge runtime configuration. It includes a secret example, but the secret is only used for local JWT verification.

low_findings.title: Cache Examples Need Adaptation For Authenticated Routes
low_findings.description: The cache examples store origin responses after fetch calls. Users should avoid copying this pattern into personalized or authenticated routes without cache key controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples are generic documentation, so impact depends on how users adapt them. The caching pattern can become unsafe if used for personalized responses.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast global applications without breaking edge runtime limits. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design practical edge middleware, caching, and authentication flows.
actual_capabilities: Compares Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge Functions, and Deno Deploy constraints.
Explains edge middleware patterns for routing, localization, and A/B testing.
Provides examples for edge authentication, JWT verification, and request forwarding.
Shows rate limiting patterns using edge storage bindings.
Demonstrates cache-first response handling for edge runtimes.
limitations: Does not deploy code or configure cloud accounts by itself.
Does not replace provider documentation for current quotas or pricing.
Examples require review before use with personalized or authenticated data.
Focuses on JavaScript and TypeScript edge runtimes.

use_cases.title: Plan Global Middleware
use_cases.description: Design request routing, geo-localization, and edge rewrites for a global web application.

use_cases.title: Review Edge Runtime Fit
use_cases.description: Compare provider limits before moving API logic from a server runtime to an edge runtime.

use_cases.title: Harden Edge APIs
use_cases.description: Add authentication, rate limiting, and cache behavior that matches edge platform constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Edge Platform
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge Functions, and Deno Deploy for my application. Include runtime limits, latency goals, and operational tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Design Edge Middleware
prompt_templates.prompt: Design edge middleware for geo-routing, A/B testing, and request headers. Explain the request flow and edge runtime constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Review Edge Caching
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this edge caching approach for privacy, cache key design, stale data, and provider-specific limitations.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Migration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration plan from a Node.js API route to an edge runtime. Include unsupported APIs, bundle constraints, testing, rollback, and monitoring.

output_examples.input: Compare edge providers for a global personalization service.
output_examples.output: A concise provider matrix with runtime limits, data storage options, latency expectations, and a recommended deployment target.

output_examples.input: Review an edge middleware design for authentication and localization.
output_examples.output: A risk-focused review covering token handling, header changes, geo behavior, fallback paths, and observability gaps.

output_examples.input: Plan rate limiting for an edge API.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step design using edge storage, request identifiers, failure behavior, and monitoring checkpoints.

best_practices: Verify current provider limits before final architecture decisions.
Keep edge bundles small and avoid unsupported Node.js APIs.
Separate public cache paths from personalized or authenticated responses.
anti_patterns: Do not assume every npm package works in an edge runtime.
Do not cache authenticated responses without explicit cache key design.
Do not move long-running or file-system-dependent work to edge functions.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy edge functions?
faq.answer: No. It guides design and review. Deployment still requires the provider CLI, project configuration, and account access.

faq.question: Which platforms does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge Functions, and Deno Deploy at a practical pattern level.

faq.question: Can it help migrate Node.js API routes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify unsupported APIs, bundle concerns, runtime limits, and migration steps.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers authentication, rate limiting, CORS, environment bindings, and caching concerns.

faq.question: Are the provider limits guaranteed current?
faq.answer: No. Provider limits change. Verify quotas, pricing, and runtime limits in official documentation.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it for implementation planning?
faq.answer: Yes. Claude Code and Codex can use it to plan edge middleware, caching, and migration work.
